title: Analytics - Software Language Change
scenario: DFU analytics event fails to display language change details (current/target language ID/name, success/failure status) when firmware update includes a software language change
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on a connected Android device.
2. Navigate to Home → [Device Name] → Device Settings.
3. Initiate a Device Firmware Update (DFU) that includes a software language change (e.g., switch from English to French).
4. Allow the DFU process to complete, including the language change.
5. Open the Firebase Analytics console and locate the DFU analytics event.
6. Verify that the event page displays default elements: graph, event count by country/gender, events per session, app version/build.

7. Verify that the event includes the following custom parameters: success/failure status, device name, firmware update status, current and target headset/charge case/charge stand versions, current and target language ID/name, headset/charge case/charge stand progress and update time in seconds, and error description (if applicable).
8. Wait a few hours for analytics data to propagate.
9. Return to the Firebase Analytics console and check the event count for the DFU language change event.
10. Confirm that the event count has incremented by at least 1.
expected: The Firebase Analytics event for DFU with language change displays all default elements and custom parameters (success/failure status, device name, firmware update status, current/target versions for headset/charge case/charge stand, current/target language ID/name, progress and update time for each component, error description). The event count increments by at least 1 after a few hours.
